title: MatMamba: A Matryoshka State Space Model
key_features: Nested Matryoshka structure within a Mamba2 block
Enables extraction of hundreds of nested submodels from the same set of weights
No additional training required during deployment

compatibility.loss_functions: any compatible with Transformer or Mamba2 layer
compatibility.learning_algorithms: any compatible with Transformer or Mamba2 layer

structure.description: A MatMamba block consists of a nested combination of Mamba2 blocks M_i, such that M_1 ⊂ M_2 ⊂ ... ⊂ M_g
structure.subset_definition: M_i ⊂ M_j means all parameters of sub-block M_i are present in M_j

training.method: g forward passes with gradient accumulation followed by a single backward pass for parameter updates
training.objective: By jointly training all granularities, the smallest sub-blocks are incentivized to represent the most important information

inference.flexibility: Any of the g nested sub-blocks M_i can be used flexibly
inference.slicing: The block can be flexibly sliced along any dimensionality (even beyond the g explicitly optimized granularities)
inference.mix_n_match: Using Mix'n'Match (Section 3.4), this operation can be performed over multiple layers at varying granularities to flexibly extract a combinatorially large number of models from the single larger model

related_work: MatFormer (Devvrit et al., 2023) - which imposes a nested structure on the FFN block in a Transformer layer
